channel.title: Comments for page "An Alchemist Goes To War"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "An Alchemist Goes To War"

item.title: Did anyone think of this?
item.content:encoded:
No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

War Alchemist lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. Love the integration of alchemy and action with a ton of very believable Foundation procedure and gear to boot. Excellent implication of character history, strong characterization, great subversion of the "found it in a flea market" trope, just great writing all around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Going to make some notes as I go along, good and bad.

+ show block
– hide block

Second paragraph has some repetition of the Agent's name, you could probably rephrase that slightly to make it smoother.

The conversation flows pretty smoothly, and the military jargon rings authentically. At least, it does to a person like me who's got a hot slice of nothing when it comes to experience in the correct use of those terms. But it at least feels right, and it works well as an establishing scene for the rest of the story.

Last two paragraphs start with the Agent Dumorne name again, so again a little bit of repetition.

looking bored

I think this might be a little stronger if you showed them doing some boredom activity to kill time. Show don't tell and all.

aequeous and aeronous aethers

I always love some added alliterative appeal.

rechecking the various bits of havoc strapped to his frame.

This is a good line. Reads real nice.

“I do not know. Probably something completely and needlessly dramatic. That’s how these things always seem to go,”

lol. Bonus points for tagging your dialogue correctly!

neatly cleaving the waiting Terronous Aetherial construct in half.

I feel like this is a little missed opportunity to inject a dash of imagery into this bit, the visualization of the big hunk of metal being ripped out was nice but this could be a cherry on top, ya dig?

If the flows could make noise, these would be screaming

Another good line.

festooned

Great vocabulary here, the thesaurus boy approves ^_^

All of the action flows well, and you do a very good job of weaving in exposition without making it drag the pace of the story down. It flows naturally in the conversation.

There are just a lot of paragraphs starting with Agent Dumornes name, enough for me to notice. A nitpick, but I think it would be better if some of them were rephrased ever so slightly.

“Flea market. I got lucky. Walked past a stall, wearing a thaumic ring. I think I had it on after the mission in Java. Started buzzing like crazy, and I picked it up immediately. “

As a flea market enthusiast I can totally see this happening all the time for the Foundation. The ending wraps up the story very nicely.


Long post collapsed.~Zyn

Overall this is highly enjoyable and I only really had nitpicks to crit it with. Well done! This deserves more looks. I'm looking at you, person scrolling through recent comments (-)_(-)

+1!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for everyone who suffered through me writing this.

With tech work, I've been…busy, and writing this has been very important to me.

A great author told me recently: "You've built so much of this world, but you don't live in it."

There was something about explosions, too.

Therefore, I do both. And now 3622 makes a lot more sense, I hope.

I wonder if anyone can identify my next article, within this one? Hmmmm?
